videos.title: NEW Claude Design Update Changes EVERYTHING!
videos.summary: Julian Goldie SEO discusses a design update to Claude, claiming it changes everything. The video promotes AI tools and courses for SEO and business automation.

videos.title: This NEW Hermes Update Builds Agents From Scratch
videos.summary: Hermes introduces a Blank Slate mode that lets users build AI agents from scratch by starting with minimal components and adding only necessary tools. This approach reduces bloat, prevents unwanted feature drift from updates, and simplifies agent configuration for lean use cases.

videos.title: Closer to the Material, Ryo Lu | Compile 26
videos.summary: Ryo Lu discusses how AI is changing the software building process and the risks of black-box abstraction. The talk emphasizes staying close to material implementation details and preserving craft rather than losing understanding through opaque AI layers. Key concern is whether AI tooling maintains human connection to the underlying system.

videos.title: This NEW NotebookLM Update Changes Everything
videos.summary: Google's NotebookLM has been upgraded to function as a full AI agent capable of writing code, building sources, and delivering finished outputs autonomously. New features include a cloud computer for handling code and documents, auto-source building from initial ideas, and a Studio for generating podcasts and reports with cited answers.

videos.title: Gemma Playground: Parallel Agents in Action
videos.summary: Ian Ballantyne demonstrates Gemma 4's multi-agent capabilities by running 10 independent subagents on a single local machine, with an orchestrator delegating tasks to subagents that autonomously complete work like SVG art generation. The demonstration achieves inference speeds above 170 tokens per second and showcases batch processing with orchestrator-and-subagent task delegation for enterprise and on-prem deployments.

videos.title: Claude Code AI SEO: How to Rank #1 with Claude Code
videos.summary: Julian Goldie presents an agentic SEO system that integrates Claude Code with a user's own Google Search Console data to identify rankable keywords and automatically generate and deploy SEO content across multiple sites. The approach bypasses common keyword tools to find less competitive opportunities, with a demonstrated case of increasing site traffic from 2 to 200+ daily clicks.

videos.title: Opening Keynote, Michael Truell | Compile 26
videos.summary: At Cursor's inaugural Compile conference, CEO Michael Truell announced Cursor Mobile, Origin Git platform, cloud agent updates, and a new model trained from scratch. The platform reports 95% usage is now agents, and Cursor is positioning itself as an agent-native development environment by giving agents their own compute resources.

videos.title: NEW Hermes Agent Updates Change Everything!
videos.summary: Hermes 0.17 introduces a framework for building teams of autonomous AI agents with async sub-agents, loop engineering for quality control, and an Agent Operating System. The update enables no-code automation and business content automation at scale.

videos.title: NEW AI SEO Agent is INSANE!
videos.summary: Julian Goldie promotes a new AI SEO agent designed for automating search engine optimization tasks. The agent is positioned as a powerful tool integrated with Agent OS and promoted through his AI Profit Lab community.

videos.title: Rethinking the ROI of cybersecurity
videos.summary: IBM Technology argues that security spending should not impede innovation. The video advocates for strategic risk thinking that balances security protection with business growth and speed to market.

videos.title: Are AI Glasses Over? + Big Technology AI Summit Audience Questions
videos.summary: Alex Kantrowitz and Ranjan Roy discuss whether AR glasses represent a viable AI product category, examining the state of AI devices, form factors, and societal implications. The episode covers Snapchat Specs, the ideal characteristics for AI devices, and geopolitical considerations in AI development.

videos.title: Gemini 3.5 Pro LEAKS is INSANE!
videos.summary: Julian Goldie SEO discusses leaked features of Gemini 3.5 Pro, characterizing them as remarkable. The video promotes his AI-related courses and services including Agent OS and AI SEO strategies.

videos.title: Get Fable 5 output on a cheaper model
videos.summary: A leaked Fable 5 system prompt can be injected into Claude Opus 4.8 to achieve near-identical output quality at a fraction of the cost. The approach requires only one line of code and is available on GitHub.

videos.title: Sakana Fugu vs Fusion (Beats Fable 5?)
videos.summary: Julian Goldie compares Sakana's Fugu (Ultra and Mini) with OpenRouter Fusion via side-by-side tests on visual and code generation tasks. Fugu Ultra ranks first for quality, Fusion second, and Fugu Mini third, though Fugu offers cheaper flat pricing with daily caps while Fusion charges per token with easier API access.

videos.title: How Gong Built a $7B+ Company in the Age of Vibe Coding | Amit Bendov
videos.summary: Amit Bendov, CEO of Gong, argues that code alone does not create lasting software advantage. Real moats come from customer context, proprietary data, product experience, and operational scale. He contends that fears of AI destroying SaaS companies are exaggerated and that the best AI products build deep customer loyalty through raving fan experiences.

videos.title: Learn 97% of Claude in Under 12 Minutes
videos.summary: Jack Roberts walks through seven levels of Claude functionality, from basic chat to autonomous agents and code generation. Coverage includes model selection between Opus and Sonnet, prompt engineering, integrations with tools like Firecrawl and Gmail, sub-agents for parallel task execution, codebase analysis with Graphify, and the Claude Code operating system for spend tracking.

videos.title: What caused this React app to crash?
videos.summary: Google for Developers presents a CI pipeline puzzle where a React app crashes in production after upgrading from React 18 to React 19, with the root cause hidden in the cache setup. The video invites viewers to diagnose the issue based on build patterns and caching behavior.

videos.title: NEW Hermes Agent v0.17.0 is INSANE
videos.summary: Hermes Agent v0.17.0, called "The Reach Release," is an open-source agent framework that integrates across applications with a new Blank Slate setup mode that lets users start with minimal configuration and add only needed features. The release includes iMessage integration, background sub-agents, persistent memory, and desktop upgrades.

videos.title: What it takes to build *realtime* voice models | Voxtral
videos.summary: Julia Turc examines Mistral's Voxtral Realtime and Voxtral TTS as examples of modern realtime voice model architecture. The video covers modular vs end-to-end speech design, audio tokenization techniques including vector quantization and finite scalar quantization, and key historical milestones like Whisper, Whisper Streaming, and WaveNet.

videos.title: AI in the SDLC: Rethinking AI Coding Tools & AI Agents
videos.summary: Cedric Clyburn argues that while AI promises speed in software development, productivity gains are limited despite faster coding. He proposes redesigning SDLC workflows with AI agents to improve testing, outcomes, and delivery.

videos.title: Stop Prompting. Start Looping (The New AI Way)
videos.summary: Loop engineering replaces manual prompt iteration by defining success criteria, letting an agent act, and using a judge model to verify results and repeat until the goal is met. The approach includes several implementations: Fusion loops with configurable rounds, agent Kanban boards with planner/builder/reviewer roles, Fusion boardroom combining multiple outputs, and Sakana Fugu council approaches.

videos.title: GLM-5.2 + OpenDesign: SOTA CHEAP DESIGN SYSTEM! This is AWESOME!
videos.summary: AICodeKing demonstrates GLM-5.2 combined with OpenDesign to generate high-quality UI prototypes including landing pages, dashboards, and mobile app interfaces. The workflow produces polished HTML and CSS artifacts with consistent design systems through integration via OpenCode and Z.AI's API setup, with OpenDesign installable via desktop app, source, or Docker.

videos.title: Apple Just Built WSL for the Mac (Container Machines)
videos.summary: Apple Container Machines is a new feature providing lightweight, persistent Linux environments on Mac, built on Apple's Container project and optimized for Apple Silicon. It functions similarly to WSL for Windows, enabling developers to test on Linux while developing on macOS with shared home directory access.

videos.title: New Agent OS is INSANE!
videos.summary: Agent OS is an operating system for coordinating multiple AI agents like Hermes and Jarvis in unified workflows. It uses Kanban boards for task triage, supports agent orchestration through group chat and pipelines, and integrates tools like Obsidian and NotebookLM for memory and content generation, with deployment options for SEO automation and other tasks.

videos.title: LIVE: Sakana VS Claude Fable 5 VS Fusion!
videos.summary: Julian Goldie tests and compares the Sakana model against Claude Fable 5 and Fusion in a live stream. The session focuses on practical AI applications built with an Agent OS framework for SEO and business automation.

videos.title: Sakana: Fable 5 Intelligence WITHOUT Fable 5!
videos.summary: Sakana released Fugu, a multi-agent orchestration system with a standard and higher-end "Fugu Ultra" variant that uses panel-style synthesis of multiple model responses. The system is accessible via a single model API and reportedly matches frontier models like Claude and others on benchmarks.

videos.title: LIVE: NEW Mythos 5.1?
videos.summary: Julian Goldie SEO promotes an Agent OS product through a live streaming session. The content focuses on building agentic operating systems and monetizing AI tools for time and cost savings.

videos.title: NEW Claude Sonnet 5 coming?
videos.summary: Julian Goldie SEO creates content discussing rumors or speculation about a potential Claude Sonnet 5 release. The video is paired with promotions for his AI business courses and SEO tools.

videos.title: China's New AI Robot MOYA Feels Too Real (92% Human)
videos.summary: A YouTube video analysis covering China's MOYA robot with lifelike skin and reactions alongside Boston Dynamics Atlas and Alibaba's Qwen-Robot. The creator argues humanoid robots are transitioning from staged demos to practical real-world tasks in factories, hospitals, and warehouses.

videos.title: Fable 5 Just Got Banned, Here’s What To Do
videos.summary: A video argues that model bans don't eliminate capability, they redirect it elsewhere. The creator suggests frontier model restrictions are temporary ecosystem friction and that alternatives like Opus 4.8 remain available and competitive.

videos.title: Delegate and schedule tasks in Claude Cowork
videos.summary: Claude Cowork is a tool that enables users to delegate work and schedule recurring tasks such as meeting prep and hourly content scans. It is presented as part of Claude's product offerings.

videos.title: 3‑2‑1 Backup Rule Explained: Protect Your Data from Disaster
videos.summary: The 3-2-1 backup rule is a data resiliency strategy that specifies maintaining three copies of data across two different media types with one copy stored offsite. This approach protects against data failures and ransomware attacks through geographic and media diversity.

videos.title: Gemma Coder (Fable Distilled): WHAT.. Fable LIKE OUTPUT from a 12B MODEL?
videos.summary: Gemma 4 12B Agentic Fable 5 Compose is a fine-tuned local coding model designed for code generation, tool use, terminal workflows, and agentic tasks. It claims approximately 3.5x improvement over base Gemma 4 12B and can be run locally via Ollama, LM Studio, or llama.cpp with Q4 K M quantization recommended.

videos.title: TypeScript is not TypeScript anymore...
videos.summary: TypeScript 7 rewrites the compiler from TypeScript into Go, achieving approximately 10 times faster type checking. The release candidate introduces shared memory parallelism, new checkers and builders flags, and a rewritten watch mode while maintaining compatibility with existing error outputs and file formats.

videos.title: 6 Things to Know about AIE World's Fair 2026
videos.summary: The AI Engineer channel announces AIE World's Fair 2026 in San Francisco, highlighting expanded content, expo floor experiences, research-industry synergy, leadership tracks, and vertical-specific sessions. The event features $40k in sponsor offers and limited early-bird ticket availability.

videos.title: This Is The First Real Shape Of AGI: Fusion Agents
videos.summary: The video argues that Fusion Agents and Apps in AI represent the first tangible shape of AGI by demonstrating the ability to execute complex workflows across multiple coordinated agents, moving beyond reasoning to actual tool-building and deployment. These systems can create interactive applications, split complex work across multiple AI workers, and deliver usable outputs integrated with real platforms.

videos.title: This OpenSource Repo will 10X Your Hermes Agent
videos.summary: Agent Reach is an open source repo that augments Hermes agents with web research capabilities across YouTube, LinkedIn, Reddit, X, and GitHub, plus data enrichment and contact verification via Clay integration. The system uses intelligent routing and multi-source research to reduce token usage while enabling automated lead discovery.

videos.title: Replace Canva with this NEW AI Tool
videos.summary: Jack Roberts demonstrates using Claude with a specialized prompt to generate Instagram carousel designs in under 10 minutes, claiming output quality matches or exceeds Canva's. The author argues this approach makes dedicated design tools unnecessary for content creation.

videos.title: GLM 5.2 is my new favorite model...
videos.summary: GLM-5.2 is an open-weights AI model licensed under MIT that ranks first on the Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index. It outperforms GPT-4.5 on the GDPval benchmark and achieved top placement on Design Arena's web design leaderboard, becoming the first model to surpass the Claude line including Claude 3.5.

videos.title: GLM 5.2 SUPER MODE: Here's HOW TO USE GLM-5.2 to it's FULL POTENTIAL
videos.summary: GLM 5.2 is presented as a major upgrade over GLM 5.1 with strong performance on coding tasks. The video demonstrates using GLM 5.2 with Verdent to build a full creator sponsorship dashboard application from a single prompt, including data persistence, responsive UI, and deployment capabilities.

videos.title: This Claude Code Plugin Writes 94% Less Code (ponytail)
videos.summary: Ponytail is a Claude Code plugin that reduces code generation by enforcing the YAGNI principle, directing AI agents to use native platform features and standard libraries before writing custom code. It claims to cut costs up to 77% while reducing code output by 94%.

videos.title: Shocking New AI Just Hit 12 Million Tokens With 1000x Less Compute
videos.summary: Subquadratic claims its SubQ model achieves 12 million token context windows with 1000x less attention compute than prior approaches using Sparse Attention Architecture (SSA). The model targets enterprise AI by enabling direct reasoning over full documents, codebases, and contracts without chunking or retrieval augmentation.

videos.title: Different Profiles, Different Personalities—A Game Changer
videos.summary: Keith AI presents content about how different profiles and personalities serve as a meaningful innovation. The video is titled "Different Profiles, Different Personalities, A Game Changer" suggesting system personalization or behavioral differentiation is impactful for AI applications.

videos.title: Best AI Logo Generator: How to Make Logo With AI (2026 Comparison)
videos.summary: A hands-on comparison of five AI logo generators: Design.com, BrandCrowd, Canva, Wix Logo Maker, and Vistaprint. The video tests each tool on the same brief to evaluate output quality, AI chat editing capabilities, licensing, and brand kit generation in 2026.

videos.title: The data black hole at the center of AI
videos.summary: Dwarkesh Patel argues that massive data volume is the central, often invisible force holding together AI capabilities. He frames data scale as analogous to a gravitational "black hole" at the center of a galaxy of model capabilities, emphasizing the gulf between human and AI sample efficiency during training.

videos.title: Save 70% of your AI Tokens
videos.summary: Graphify is an open source tool that maps a codebase into a knowledge graph, allowing AI systems to reference the graph structure instead of re-reading the entire codebase with each prompt. This approach reduces token consumption by up to 70 percent while improving answer accuracy.

videos.title: Stop Prompting Claude. Start Loop Engineering.
videos.summary: Loop Engineering (or Agentic Engineering) is a technique where developers build iterative loops with AI agents instead of relying on single-prompt interactions. The approach requires five building blocks: trigger, execution skills, goal and verification, and output and memory, enabling AI systems to autonomously work toward objectives.

videos.title: One Shared #Hermes From Any Device (Local UI, VPS Brain)
videos.summary: Keith AI demonstrates a distributed architecture where Hermes can be shared across multiple devices using a local UI connected to a remote VPS-hosted inference backend. This enables local interface access while centralizing model compute resources.

videos.title: Why Desktop #Hermes Beats #Telegram for Real Work
videos.summary: Keith AI argues that Desktop Hermes outperforms Telegram for productive work. The video covers sessions, profiles, and artifacts as key features distinguishing Hermes.

videos.title: Microsoft’s new AI models & bots dominate the internet
videos.summary: A Cloudflare study shows agentic AI bots generate 57.4% of web requests globally. The Mixture of Experts podcast panel debates whether this represents a fundamental shift in web interaction or a continuation of existing bot dominance patterns.

videos.title: ego lite: I gave Claude Code & Codex a browser to run web automation — here's what happened
videos.summary: ego lite is a Chromium-based browser designed for AI agents to perform web automation in isolated workspaces. It enables agents like Claude, Codex, and Cursor to handle web workflows through code-based composition and semantic page snapshots while maintaining separation from the user's normal browsing context.

videos.title: Master Hermes Desktop in 34 mins
videos.summary: A tutorial on setting up and using Hermes Desktop, a control center for managing AI agents with features including local LLM integration, VPS deployment, remote gateway access via Tailscale, profiles, sessions, sub-agents, and cron blueprints. The video demonstrates switching from Telegram-based control to a dedicated desktop application for better workflow organization.

videos.title: Microsoft And DeepSeek Are Launching New AI Together
videos.summary: Microsoft has made Copilot Cowork generally available worldwide with usage-based billing. The platform is being positioned as a multi-model AI agent platform that can route enterprise work across different models including OpenAI, Claude, Cowork 1, and potentially DeepSeek on Azure. Microsoft is also introducing Web IQ for AI agents within the platform.

videos.title: Gemma 4 and the AI Edge Gallery: On-Device AI Gets an Upgrade
videos.summary: Google announced Gemma 4, a language model available through the AI Edge Gallery mobile app for on-device inference. The app achieved over five million model downloads in its first month and supports offline use cases including text generation and audio-to-structured-output tasks. The 2B variant this year matches performance of last year's 27B model.

videos.title: Stop Claude from forgetting anything
videos.summary: Hermes is a desktop application that preserves Claude's context and memories across sessions, eliminating the need to re-explain project details. It builds a skill library incrementally as users work, providing persistent memory across projects like a coworker with perfect recall.

videos.title: Hermes Agent Tutorial: How to Setup & Use Open Source AI Agent for Beginners
videos.summary: Hermes Agent is an open-source AI agent with nearly 200K GitHub stars that runs locally on Mac or Windows. It features persistent memory, skill self-improvement, Telegram integration, and cron job automation for tasks like daily news briefs, requiring no paid subscriptions or servers.

videos.title: Matt Pocock’s Agentic Engineering Workflow (just copy him)
videos.summary: David Ondrej discusses Matt Pocock's agentic engineering workflow and recommends adopting it as a best practice. The video features practical guidance on coding with AI and includes tooling recommendations such as SerpApi for web search integration.

videos.title: Gemma Playground: AI Edge Gallery
videos.summary: Ian Ballantyne demonstrates Gemma running on Pixel 10 Pro using the Google AI Edge Gallery app, showcasing on-device capabilities including image understanding, audio transcription, agent routing, and multimodal inference without connectivity. The app is available on Android and iOS.

videos.title: 3 patterns to build long-running AI agents
videos.summary: Addy Osmani outlines three core architectural patterns for production-grade long-running AI agents: event-driven dormancy for efficient idle management, durable checkpointing for state persistence, and separated evaluation for robust execution. These patterns enable multi-day workflows beyond stateless chatbots.

videos.title: Artifacts in Claude Code: share your work as it happens
videos.summary: Artifacts in Claude Code is a feature that transforms raw outputs like data, mockups, and JSON into clear visual pages shareable with teams. Currently available in beta on Team and Enterprise plans.

videos.title: Antigravity SDK: Building a digital simulated world
videos.summary: Google Antigravity SDK enables building multi-agent social simulations with virtual avatars interacting autonomously in simulated environments. A demonstration at Google I/O created a simulation inside a virtual space station with attendee avatars.

videos.title: Enterprise-managed auth for MCP connectors
videos.summary: Claude has released enterprise-managed authentication for MCP connectors, allowing administrators to authorize and authenticate connectors organization-wide through identity providers without requiring individual employee setup. The feature simplifies onboarding from admin configuration through employee login.

videos.title: 3 takeaways on Software Ecology™️
videos.summary: Google for Developers video presents three takeaways on Software Ecology and explores what easy AI code generation means for the future of software systems. Speaker Adam Bender discusses the implications using Google AI products.

videos.title: Karpathy Bigram explained in 10min..
videos.summary: Caleb Writes Code presents Andrej Karpathy's explanation of bigram language models as a foundation for understanding larger language models like GPT. The video covers essential components including tokenization, embeddings, loss functions, softmax, and optimization with AdamW.

videos.title: 7 tips for using Antigravity 2.0 on enterprise codebases, coding phase
videos.summary: Google Cloud Tech demonstrates practical workflows for deploying Antigravity 2.0, an AI coding agent, on enterprise codebases. The video covers seven tactics including encoding team practices into agent skills, automated test-driven self-correction, UI validation via headless browsers, and asynchronous background task offloading.

videos.title: Your AI Agent Is Missing Half the Internet… Until Now (Agent-Reach)
videos.summary: Agent-Reach is an open-source tool that enables AI agents to access real internet context across Western and Chinese platforms including Twitter/X, Reddit, YouTube, GitHub, Bilibili, and Xiaohongshu. It addresses the limitation that coding agents struggle when they need information from discussions, social feedback, tutorials, and non-English research sources outside their codebase.

videos.title: wtf is Loop Engineer & how to setup for real
videos.summary: AI Jason explains Loop Engineer, a technique for setting up AI agents. The video includes a free course on building AI agents, links to a GitHub template, and a workshop for learning the approach in practical context.

videos.title: How to build an AI Agent and MCP Server (step-by-step)
videos.summary: Model Context Protocol is an open standard that connects AI agents to external tools and live data sources. Google demonstrates MCP usage through its Agent Development Kit (ADK), showing how to integrate external data sources like Google Trends with AI agents using MCP servers.

videos.title: The Production AI Playbook: Deploying Agents at Enterprise Scale — Sandipan Bhaumik, Databricks
videos.summary: Sandipan Bhaumik from Databricks presents a five-pillar production framework for enterprise AI agents: evaluation, observability, data foundation, multi-agent orchestration, and governance. The talk uses a retail banking case study where initial PoC failure was traced to stale embeddings after policy updates, demonstrating the critical need for comprehensive tracing and monitoring systems in production AI systems.

videos.title: Build a Real-World Multi-Agent AI App with MCP + LangGraph | Part 2
videos.summary: Tutorial on building a multi-agent travel planning system by integrating LangGraph with MCP servers for real-time flight and weather data. The system uses PostgreSQL for memory, Tavily search, external APIs, and Streamlit for the UI.

videos.title: Building AI Agents for Real-World Problems & Workflows
videos.summary: IBM Technology explains how AI agents operate in production systems with workflows, policies, and orchestration. The video discusses agent coordination, task enforcement, rule application, and alignment with human decision-making in real-world deployments.

videos.title: FULLY FREE GLM-5.2 + Z-Code: This is ACTUALLY GOOD!
videos.summary: Z AI launched Z Code, a Codex-like coding agent for GLM models with a free tier offering 5 million tokens per day. The tool supports code generation, project creation, element inspection, DevTools integration, and remote platform connections, though it lacks a file explorer, changelog view, worktree support, and one-click git initialization.

videos.title: 6 INSANE Claude Cowork Use Cases to Save Hours (AI Workflow Automation)
videos.summary: Tutorial demonstrating six automation workflows using Claude Cowork, including morning briefs, content generation, email triage, research reports, dashboards, and desktop control agents. The video positions Cowork as a workflow automation platform beyond simple chatbot usage.

videos.title: When you realize that you’re the senior dev now.
videos.summary: A Google for Developers video featuring Pete King discussing the experience of becoming a senior developer. The piece reflects on career advancement and the responsibilities that come with seniority in software development.

videos.title: How to build a custom vision agent
videos.summary: Google Developer Expert John Capobianco demonstrates building a custom vision agent that captures live photos from a webcam and animates them. The system uses Gemini, Model Context Protocol, and processes natural language prompts and real-time American Sign Language conversations.

videos.title: The Creators of Claude Code and OpenClaw don't Prompt Their Agents Anymore?!
videos.summary: Top AI engineers use automated loops to repeatedly prompt agents instead of manual prompting. An orchestrator agent spins up worker agents in parallel, with the system storing all runs in a database and providing observability dashboards. This approach is more cost-effective than relying solely on expensive frontier models.

videos.title: China’s New AI Is 6X More Efficient Than Claude
videos.summary: Moonshot AI released Kimi K2.7 Code, an open-weight model designed for coding agents that work through real codebases. The model is positioned as efficient and capable for real developer workflows competing with closed systems.

videos.title: From Idea to Code: Building with Miro and Kiro — Live
videos.summary: A live session demonstrating how Miro and Kiro work together to move from idea to working code. Seán Winters and Brian Beach show visual collaboration with AI-assisted development through prototyping and building, with examples from hackathons in Paris, Amsterdam, LA, and New York.

videos.title: Every Level of Hermes Agent Explained
videos.summary: A tutorial series explaining seven levels of Hermes agent complexity, from basic prompting to fully autonomous systems that orchestrate multiple LLMs and execute scheduled tasks. Covers integration with Claude, ChatGPT, DeepSeek, and Grok through a unified framework.

videos.title: Converting SQL Server types and rules to PostgreSQL
videos.summary: Google Cloud demonstrates using Gemini as an AI tutor to convert SQL Server types and rules to PostgreSQL. The tool helps developers migrate database schemas and explains conversion processes like converting user-defined types to PostgreSQL DOMAINs.

videos.title: Microsoft's launched OpenClaw?
videos.summary: Microsoft Scout is an AI agent integrated into Microsoft's ecosystem (Teams, email, calendar) that proactively prepares work materials and takes actions before users request them, functioning like an autonomous chief of staff. The tool learns from plain language descriptions of work and anticipates needs across Microsoft's productivity suite.

videos.title: Coding with ADK to beat a text adventure game
videos.summary: A Google Cloud livestream demonstration on using the Agent Development Kit (ADK) to build an AI agent capable of playing and solving a text adventure game. Speakers include Tilde Thurium and Abdel Sghiouar.

videos.title: AI Fact or Fiction: The Fable Ban, Tokenmaxxing, Saaspocolypse — With Ara Kharazian
videos.summary: Ara Kharazian, lead economist at Ramp, discusses whether companies are spending meaningfully on AI and whether that spending generates real value. The episode examines market narratives including Anthropic's competitive gains over OpenAI, tokenmaxxing, the DeepSeek resurgence, and predictions of a SaaS apocalypse.

videos.title: I Tried the Open Source ElevenLabs Alternative (Voicebox)
videos.summary: Voicebox is a free open-source local AI voice studio for developers offering voice cloning, text-to-speech, dictation, and REST API integration as a privacy-focused alternative to cloud services like ElevenLabs. The video demonstrates its capabilities including voice cloning, system dictation, and integration with tools like Claude Code.

videos.title: Your Agent's Biggest Lie: "I Searched the Web" — Rafael Levi, Bright Data
videos.summary: Agents often fail to search the web due to blocks, CAPTCHAs, or stale data but return answers as if successful. Using Bright Data's Web MCP enables real web access, improving reliability, citations, real-time results, and reducing hallucinations compared to agents without proper web connectivity.

videos.title: How to Start a Business Using AI: From Idea to Launch in One Day
videos.summary: AI Master evaluates Rocket 1.0 as a solution to the problem of losing context across disconnected AI tools in typical workflows. The reviewer tests Rocket's ability to integrate research, notes, code, and tracking in a unified system, examining its competitive intelligence, MVP building, and team collaboration features alongside competing approaches like Claude Code.

videos.title: AI agents can manage your passwords. Should we let them? Plus: The biggest Patch Tuesday ever.
videos.summary: A podcast episode discussing whether AI agents should manage passwords and detect compromised credentials, examining cybersecurity hygiene practices. It covers Microsoft's largest Patch Tuesday with 200+ CVEs and explores C-suite executives' willingness to accept cyber risk for innovation gains.

videos.title: Minimax Code + M3 : Crazy Alternative to Claude,Codex!
videos.summary: MiniMax M3 is an open-weights AI model designed for coding agents, long-context workflows, and multimodality. It supports up to one million token context windows and is compatible with both Anthropic and OpenAI API endpoints. MiniMax Code includes agent team features for collaborative task implementation and verification.

videos.title: POV: A new feature enters the chat. 🕺✨
videos.summary: Google for Developers released a lighthearted video titled "POV: A new feature enters the chat" featuring speakers Justine Lai, M.E Francis, and Pete King. The content humorously sympathizes with software engineers' experiences managing new feature releases.

videos.title: New #1 open-source AI model is here!
videos.summary: AI Search reviews GLM 5.2, claiming it as the new top open-source AI model. The review covers various capabilities including 3D model generation, animation with voiceover, music composition, and math animation, along with benchmark comparisons and open-source availability.

videos.title: Fable 5 Replacement Just Dropped: Fusion (Fable Level AI)
videos.summary: OpenRouter introduced Fusion, a system that routes a single prompt to multiple AI models, compares their outputs, identifies gaps, and synthesizes them into a unified response. A budget configuration reportedly matched Fable 5 performance on deep research benchmarks at roughly half the cost.

videos.title: Agent Toolkit for AWS Explained in 2 Minutes
videos.summary: Agent Toolkit for AWS is a framework that equips AI coding agents with tools, documentation, best practices, and guardrails for working with AWS services. It provides access to up-to-date AWS knowledge and procedures to improve agent success rates and reduce inefficient attempts.

videos.title: The Briefing: Financial Services
videos.summary: Major banks are deploying Claude across their organizations. Anthropic hosted a virtual briefing session featuring leaders from these institutions discussing their implementations and use cases in financial services.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 with Harness Engineering & Loops Will Do Everything.
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof covers Claude Fable 5 and discusses harness engineering, AI loops, and their applications in regulated industries. The video explores tensions between open and closed source AI models, risks of model shutdowns, and the state of AI-powered SaaS, drawing on experience since 2023 in the space.

videos.title: Running Claude Code on Amazon Bedrock
videos.summary: AWS Bedrock enables developers to run Claude Code with data residency compliance and cost control. The service includes cross-region inference, prompt caching, the Mantle endpoint for efficient routing, per-team cost tracking, and Guardrails for content governance.

videos.title: Building long-running AI agents with ADK
videos.summary: Google Cloud Tech tutorial on building long-running AI agents using the Agent Development Kit (ADK), an open source framework. The video covers the long-running agent pattern with stateless, event-driven systems and multi-agent delegation through coordinator agents managing sub-agents.

videos.title: Better Free AI Image than Midjourney?
videos.summary: Jack Roberts reviews Ideogram 4.0, claiming it excels at rendering text within generated images compared to Midjourney and other models. He notes it beats competitors on certain leaderboards like DesignArena and is available for free.

videos.title: Machiavelli is the most misunderstood thinker of all time – Ada Palmer
videos.summary: Ada Palmer discusses how Machiavelli is widely misunderstood, explaining his career as a diplomat for Florence and how his experiences observing European rulers informed his later writings on power and politics. Palmer draws a parallel between Machiavelli's reluctance to share his book widely and Szilard's warning to FDR about nuclear weapons, both motivated by concern for their homelands.

videos.title: Building with ADK & A2A: How multi-agent collaboration designs a game
videos.summary: Google Cloud Tech demonstrates multi-agent collaboration using Agent Development Kit (ADK) and Agent2Agent protocol (A2A) to design a game. Multiple AI agents specialize in architecture, asset rendering, and project management with human-in-the-loop oversight.

videos.title: "ChatGPT Moment" for Robotics Is Coming. The Real Problem Isn't Intelligence | Stanford, Catie Cuan
videos.summary: Catie Cuan argues that a "ChatGPT Moment" is coming for robotics but that humanoid robot development may be myopic. She contends that the real challenge is not intelligence but designing robots for delight and human interaction rather than utility alone, and that billions of robots will soon enter society requiring thoughtful design philosophy.

videos.title: Is Unstructured Data The Key To Successful AI Deployments?
videos.summary: Hyland leadership and Erie Insurance's CIO discuss how unstructured data is critical to successful enterprise AI deployments and agentic systems. The discussion covers why prior AI pilots have failed due to token allotment limitations and how proper data handling drives business impact across healthcare, banking, and insurance sectors.

videos.title: You Might Not Need 50 Diffusion Steps — Ziv Ilan, Nvidia
videos.summary: NVIDIA's approach combines dynamic quantization, latent caching, and step distillation to reduce diffusion model denoising from fifty steps to one to eight steps, achieving real-time video generation on Blackwell B200. The techniques are modular and composable, with trajectory-based and distribution-based distillation approaches for training student models.

videos.title: Gemini Omni Tutorial: How to Use Gemini Omni AI for Beginners
videos.summary: A YouTube tutorial demonstrating Google Gemini Omni (Omni Flash) for AI video generation and editing workflows. The video tests physics simulation, character consistency, scene memory, and object manipulation, comparing performance against Sora and examining where the model excels in iterative editing versus its current limitations.

videos.title: Kagenti’s Approach to Multi-Agent Security for AI Agents
videos.summary: Kagenti addresses security risks in multi-agent AI systems by implementing identity-based security with delegation chains and open source infrastructure. The approach prevents data leaks and invisible breaches in production agentic AI deployments.

videos.title: This command SAVED my broken AI coding session
videos.summary: A YouTube video from JavaScript Mastery describes a command that resolved issues in an AI-assisted coding workflow. The specific command and its mechanics are detailed in the full video.

videos.title: Tencent Workbuddy: RIP Claude,Codex! This is ACTUALLY quite a bit CRAZY!
videos.summary: Tencent WorkBuddy is a desktop AI agent designed for office productivity tasks including PDF reading, spreadsheet analysis, report creation, and presentation generation. It features 100+ domain expert agents, multi-agent workflows, remote control via messaging apps, and integrations with tools like GitHub, Jira, and Google Drive.

videos.title: Run Gemma on the edge with the Coral Board
videos.summary: Google's Coral Board is a low-power edge dev board with an integrated Coral NPU accelerator designed for on-device AI inference. It runs Gemma models and includes peripherals such as a screen, camera, microphones, and LEDs for complete edge AI applications.

videos.title: Google Just Revealed What Comes After AGI And It’s Shocking
videos.summary: A YouTube video discussing Google DeepMind's "From AGI to ASI" paper, which argues that the transition from AGI to artificial superintelligence may be more consequential than reaching AGI. The video emphasizes that once human-level AI can be copied, accelerated, and connected into agent teams to improve itself, intelligence could become an industrialized process.

videos.title: Stop testing your SAAS manually
videos.summary: KaneAI is an automated QA testing tool that accepts plain English descriptions of test cases, automatically generates and runs tests, and reports results. It aims to reduce testing overhead and costs by automating work traditionally done by QA teams.

videos.title: This MCP makes Hermes Agent 10x more powerful
videos.summary: David Ondrej demonstrates Apify MCP Connectors that enhance the Hermes Agent system. The connectors enable integration with web data sources through a Model Context Protocol interface, with a Universal MCP Connector available for broader compatibility.

videos.title: Are senior developers going back to writing code by hand?
videos.summary: A discussion between Smitha Kolan and Addy Osmani on whether AI coding agents add technical debt or value. The video argues that human verification, rigorous code review, and maintaining engineering craft are essential to ensure AI-accelerated development reduces maintenance costs rather than inflating output speed.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 is Banned... Do THIS Right Now
videos.summary: A tutorial on running large language models locally using Ollama and building a free NotebookLM alternative with open notebook. The video addresses concerns about cloud model availability by showing how to deploy and route models across local and cloud services including Claude, DeepSeek, and OpenRouter.

videos.title: SpaceX’s IPO Triumph, Anthropic’s Fable Fumble, OpenAI’s Price War
videos.summary: A tech news discussion covering SpaceX's IPO and its implications for AI competitors, Anthropic's Fable model rollout challenges, OpenAI's potential price reductions, and broader questions about whether AI models are becoming commodities. The hosts debate the strategic positioning and valuation of major AI companies.

videos.title: The magic behind GenLatte: Flutter, Firebase, and Gemini
videos.summary: GenLatte is a generative UI demo application showcased at Google Cloud Next '26 that uses Gemini, Flutter, and Firebase to generate personalized latte artwork. The app demonstrates agentic features and Agent-to-UI capabilities to create dynamic, on-demand user experiences tailored to individual preferences.

videos.title: Omnigent: The New Meta-Harness for EVERY Coding Agent - Claude Code, Codex, Pi, More
videos.summary: Omnigent is a meta-harness that orchestrates multiple coding agents (Claude Code, Codex, Pi) as interchangeable workers. It enables routing tasks to different models based on suitability, includes human-in-the-loop governance, and maintains sessions across devices. The framework is open source and designed to help engineers use the right model for each job.

videos.title: Why MCP and ChatGPT Apps Use Double Iframes — Frédéric Barthelet, Alpic
videos.summary: Double iframe architecture isolates MCP and ChatGPT apps by nesting an inner srcdoc iframe inside an outer iframe served from a controlled subdomain. This approach avoids Content Security Policy violations, localStorage/cookie access vulnerabilities, and sandbox escapes that simpler isolation methods suffer from. The pattern originates from Facebook's app marketplace and requires apps to declare all external domains in metadata.

videos.title: In production, which file actually gets loaded?
videos.summary: A Google Developers educational video by Christine Rafla explores which file gets loaded in production when the same package import behaves differently after deployment. The video examines the gap between application code visibility and actual runtime file resolution in production environments.

videos.title: What Is AI Pair Programming? AI Coding Tools for Developers
videos.summary: IBM Technology presents an overview of AI pair programming, a practice where AI tools assist developers by automating debugging, code review, and enhancing developer productivity. The content demonstrates how AI acts as a collaborative teammate in real development workflows.

videos.title: FULLY FREE Unlimited API + OpenCode: MiniMax M3,Step 3.7 Flash,Nemotron 3 Ultra,GLM,Kimi!
videos.summary: OpenRouter has introduced Fusion API, a compound model system that combines responses from multiple models by sending prompts to a panel and using a judge model to produce the final answer. The creator tests whether it lives up to OpenRouter's claims of matching Fable-level intelligence at lower cost, finding inconsistent real-world performance across simulators, SVG generation, math, and coding tasks.

videos.title: Why I never trust fully generated Code
videos.summary: JavaScript Mastery author expresses distrust in fully generated code from AI systems. The piece represents a viewpoint on code generation reliability and best practices for developers.

videos.title: The Internet Is No Longer Human
videos.summary: Web bots now outnumber human requests, Google AI Overviews reduce click-through rates, and AI agents are beginning to perform autonomous actions like shopping and browsing on behalf of users. The video argues that internet infrastructure designed for humans is being repurposed for machine access, with implications for content creators, websites, and the future role of human internet users.

videos.title: STOP Using Claude Code Without This Fable 5 Agentic OS
videos.summary: Chase AI presents a tutorial on building an agentic operating system using Claude Code, covering architecture, memory management, observability, and skill customization to optimize Claude Code efficiency and agency development.

videos.title: You Don't Need React Portals For Modals Anymore (react-call)
videos.summary: react-call is a lightweight React utility that enables calling components as asynchronous functions, eliminating the need for React Portals in modal implementations. It provides architectural patterns for layout isolation, singleton management, and state mutation flows without requiring context providers or complex state tracking.

videos.title: Paste This Into Claude, It'll Make You Build 10x Faster.
videos.summary: Austin Marchese presents six "power phrases" for Claude Code that allegedly accelerate development velocity. He claims these phrases, endorsed by Claude Code's creator and Andrej Karpathy, improve project setup, scaling, and automation without requiring smarter models or better prompts.

videos.title: Smart AI routing explained
videos.summary: IBM Technology highlights a shift in AI labs from maximizing model size to implementing smart routing strategies. The approach prioritizes cost efficiency and trustworthiness by intelligently directing tasks across different models rather than defaulting to the largest available model.

videos.title: How to ACTUALLY Use AI For Coding
videos.summary: OpenAI's Codex introduces a /goal command paired with a "Ralph Loop" that allows developers to describe desired outcomes in plain English rather than providing step-by-step instructions. The system autonomously plans steps, executes them, tests output, and self-corrects without user intervention.

videos.title: Bridging Design Systems and Code with MCP and AI Agents
videos.summary: Context engineering is a technique that guides AI agents in software design by leveraging design systems. MCP enables AI agents to work together with design systems to build consistent and correct applications.

videos.title: Build a Hermes Knowledge Base That Self-Improves
videos.summary: A tutorial on building a self-improving knowledge base for the Hermes AI agent using Obsidian wiki, Claude, and NotebookLM. The workflow ingests meeting notes, emails, and research via automated cron jobs with Granola, implementing retrieval-augmented generation principles inspired by Andrej Karpathy's LLM wiki methodology to overcome LLM context limitations.

videos.title: OpenRouter Fusion: They OFFICIALLY CLAIM THAT THIS MODEL beats FABLE?
videos.summary: AICodeKing reviews OpenRouter's Fusion API, which uses multiple models in parallel with a judge model to route responses. While OpenRouter claims Fable-level performance at lower cost, testing shows inconsistent or poor results in simulators, SVG generation, math, and coding tasks, often with higher latency and cost than single strong models.

videos.title: RIP Claude Fable, open-source AI unleashed, full body avatars, new Google models, new TTS: AI NEWS
videos.summary: AI news video covering recent AI model releases including GLM 5.2, Kimi K2.7, MiniMax M3, DiffusionGemma, and new tools for agents, video generation, avatar synthesis, and text-to-speech. Video also reviews Claude Fable and discusses various open-source AI models and research projects across vision, language, and multimodal domains.

videos.title: 3,000+ Tokens Per Second? Let's Stress Test Xiaomi's New Model: MiMo V2.5 Pro UltraSpeed
videos.summary: Xiaomi and TileRT released a 1-trillion-parameter Mixture-of-Experts model achieving over 1,000 tokens-per-second throughput on standard hardware. The model combines selective FP4 quantization, DFlash speculative decoding, and TileRT persistent GPU kernel runtime to optimize inference speed and reduce bottlenecks.

videos.title: Fable 5 is never coming back, here's why
videos.summary: David Ondrej reports that Fable 5 has been banned and frames this as the beginning of a permanent underclass. The video appears to discuss consequences of game bans or platform restrictions in relation to open-source AI and broader technological access.

videos.title: This Is Bad... They Just Shut Down FABLE 5
videos.summary: Anthropic's Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models were shut down by US government order after only three days of public access. The video raises concerns about whether frontier AI systems will become restricted to government control or remain accessible to the public.

videos.title: GPT-6 Is Launching Into a World OpenAI No Longer Controls
videos.summary: A video essay arguing that OpenAI's competitive advantage has eroded as Microsoft, Apple, and Google deployed alternative models. The analysis covers rumored GPT-6 delays, Claude 3.5 Fable benchmarks exceeding GPT-5.5, and major tech platforms switching away from OpenAI's models in key applications.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 is Now BANNED?!

videos.title: 1,000+ Tokens/Sec: Google Just Shattered the AI Speed Limit (DiffusionGemma)
videos.summary: Google DeepMind released DiffusionGemma, a text generation model that applies diffusion-based image generation techniques to language by using uniform state diffusion to iteratively refine token predictions over bidirectional passes. The architecture shifts local LLMs from memory-bound to compute-bound, enabling generation speeds exceeding 1,000 tokens per second on H100 GPUs, though with quality tradeoffs compared to standard autoregressive models.

videos.title: This AI Chinese model is 15X cheaper?
videos.summary: MiniMax M3 is a long-context AI model deployed on OpenRouter that uses sparse attention to process long documents 15.6x faster than baseline while reducing compute costs to one-twentieth of the previous generation. The model intelligently focuses on relevant portions of input and ignores irrelevant content.

videos.title: GLM-5.2 (Fully Tested): I got EARLY ACCESS & This MODEL is CRAZY!
videos.summary: GLM-5.2 is an updated model from Z AI featuring a 1 million token context window, planned open-weight release, and strong coding performance. It achieves approximately 81 percent benchmark score, roughly 6 percent below Opus 4.8, and is offered at USD 8 per month through the GLM Coding Plan.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 Now Banned in Europe by US Government (EU-Breakdown)
videos.summary: A video by Cyril Imhof discusses the claimed ban of Claude Fable 5 in Europe by the US government. The video references regulatory developments concerning Anthropic's model access across EU jurisdictions.

videos.title: Run 6–7 Claude Windows at Once (CLI Power Move)

videos.title: Bypass Permissions Mode: Dangerous, But Also Very Cool

videos.title: Claude’s “Safety vs Autonomy” Dial Explained

videos.summary: Google Antigravity is an agent-first software development platform that enables developers to build, orchestrate, and monitor AI agents. It offers multiple surfaces including a desktop app (Antigravity 2.0), IDE, CLI, and SDK for integrating agents into development workflows.

videos.title: If you don’t run Pi locally you’re falling behind…

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 is smarter💡
videos.summary: IBM Technology covers Claude Fable 5, highlighting improvements in planning, code analysis, and inference speed. The segment is part of the Mixture of Experts episode series.

videos.title: Claude FM 🎵 music for thinking and building
videos.summary: Claude FM is a music streaming service made and curated by musicians, designed to provide ambient music for thinking and building. It is available on YouTube and branded by Anthropic's Claude.

videos.title: Converting SQL Server code to PostgreSQL

videos.title: How I make my AI Agent remember everything
videos.summary: JavaScript Mastery covers techniques for enabling AI agents to retain and recall information across interactions. The content demonstrates practical implementation approaches for agent memory systems.

videos.title: The $20,000 API Key Mistake

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 & Apple’s NVIDIA deal
videos.summary: A podcast episode discusses Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 release and its tier routing system, Apple's NVIDIA partnership announced at WWDC 2026 abandoning on-device AI, and the capability of AI models to detect sarcasm through multimodal inputs and context.

videos.title: The “Final Boss” of AI Workflows: UltraCode
videos.summary: Keith AI discusses UltraCode as a workflow system framed as the "Final Boss" of AI workflows. The video covers UltraCode Dynamic Workflows as a core feature.

videos.title: Claude Code (Fable) V/S Codex (GPT-5.5) : MATHEMATICAL TEST = ONE CLEAR WINNER!

videos.summary: Claude Fable 5 faces backlash over excessive refusal of harmless prompts, invisible guardrails, and potential fallback to weaker models. The controversy centers on whether users can trust the actual model output versus hidden safety-driven modifications by Anthropic.

videos.title: The Ultimate Second Brain for AI Entrepreneurs: Fable 5 Claude with Loops
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof discusses Fable 5, an Anthropic model designed for AI entrepreneurs, highlighting its features, guardrails, memory capabilities, and agentic payment integration compared to Mythos 5.

videos.summary: Google Cloud Tech tutorial on building AI-powered agents using the Agent Development Kit, Model Context Protocol, and Looker. Covers MCP configuration, tool definitions, API credential setup, and creating agents that translate prompts into data requests via Looker.

The pipeline involves reverse-engineering successful channels, writing scripts, recording voice-overs, and using Claude to automatically edit videos together into finished YouTube content.

videos.title: Why Can't Anyone Answer Questions About the Business? — Garrett Galow, WorkOS
videos.summary: Garrett Galow from WorkOS presented Studio, an internal workspace where non-technical users can ask natural language questions against data sources like Snowflake, Linear, and Notion. Studio uses LLM-generated declarative JavaScript widgets that are validated before deployment, making queries deterministic and reusable across the organization.

videos.summary: Cursor's Sam Whitmore discusses with Baseten representatives approaches to running coding agents at scale. Topics include running agents in large parallel batches, inter-agent communication and review mechanisms, and moving from simple parallelism to coordinated multi-agent systems.

The conversation covers how AI assistants would integrate into daily tasks, on-device AI, competitive dynamics with OpenAI, Google, Apple, and Amazon, and privacy concerns around facial recognition technology.

videos.title: Local AI Agents Masterclass (Gemma 4 + OpenCode + Odysseus tutorial)

The setup uses Gemma 4 via Ollama for local inference with Venice API as fallback, deployed in Docker for privacy-focused local execution.

videos.title: 1,000 AI agents, 0 LLM calls?
videos.summary: A tutorial on optimizing AI agent architectures to run at scale with minimal LLM calls by combining deterministic logic with agent decision-making. It covers when to use LLMs versus deterministic algorithms, callback hooks to bypass model calls, and route planning using the Spine and Sprout algorithm, demonstrating 1,000 agents running with zero LLM invocations.

videos.title: 21 Hermes Lessons to Get You Started
videos.summary: Jack Roberts presents 21 lessons or use cases for Hermes, a language model. The content is educational guidance on getting started with the model.

videos.summary: WebMCP is a proposed web standard by Google that enables AI agents to interact with websites through structured tools instead of DOM parsing and screenshot-based reasoning. videos.title: What is MCP? Learn MCP Client, MCP Server & MCP Architecture
videos.summary: Model Context Protocol (MCP) is a standard that enables AI agents and applications to connect with external tools, APIs, databases, and services through a common framework. The video explains MCP's client-server architecture, communication mechanisms using JSON-RPC, and local versus remote deployment options for use in AI agent systems.

videos.title: Your Attention Is the Bottleneck, Not Your Agents — Zack Proser, WorkOS
videos.summary: Zack Proser argues that human attention, not agent speed, is the bottleneck in AI-augmented development. He proposes a four-layer stack: signal agents for notifications, verification gates for safety, weekly history analysis for skill gaps, and biometric feedback loops. The system prioritizes sustainable developer cognition over agent parallelism.

videos.title: Gemini Omni's Best Feature
videos.summary: Google's Gemini Omni can add objects to existing video footage by accepting text, image, or audio descriptions and automatically handling lighting and shading. It enables real-time post-production-style object insertion into existing clips.

videos.title: "An endless demand for compute" | Jonathan Ross, founder of Groq

videos.title: AI That’s Too Dangerous For You? What we learned from S.A.T.A.N

videos.title: The AI Skill I use to prevent refactors

Key features include slash commands, plan mode, headless mode, checkpoints, PR review, MCP support, IDE integration, and adaptive learning from user edits.

videos.title: I One-Shotted An Entire App With Claude Fable 5 (Full Breakdown)
